2605.19932.title: PEEK: Context Map as an Orientation Cache for Long-Context LLM Agents
2605.19932.paper_summary: Large language model (LLM) agents increasingly operate over long and recurring external contexts, like document corpora and code repositories. Across invocations, existing approaches preserve either the agent's trajectory, passive access to raw material, or task-level strategies. None of them preserves what we argue is most needed for repeated same-context workloads: reusable orientation knowledge (e.g., what the context contains, how it is organized, and which entities, constants, and schemas have historically been useful) about the recurring context itself. We introduce PEEK, a system that caches and maintains this orientation knowledge as a context map: a small, constant-sized artifact in the agent's prompt that gives it a persistent peek into the external context. The map is maintained by a programmable cache policy with three modules: a Distiller that extracts transferable knowledge from inference-time signals, a Cartographer that translates it into structured edits, and a priority-based Evictor that enforces a fixed token budget. On long-context reasoning and information aggregation, PEEK improves over strong baselines by 6.3-34.0% while using 93-145 fewer iterations and incurring 1.7-5.8x lower cost than the state-of-the-art prompt-learning framework, ACE. On context learning, PEEK improves solving rate and rubric accuracy by 6.0-14.0% and 7.8-12.1%, respectively, at 1.4x lower cost than ACE. These gains generalize across LMs and agent architectures, including OpenAI Codex, a production-grade coding agent. Together, these results show that a context map helps long-context LLM agents interact with recurring external contexts more accurately and efficiently.
2605.19932.paper_summary_zh: 大型語言模型（LLM）代理越來越多地在長期和重複的外部上下文中運作，例如文檔語料庫和代碼庫。現有的方法在多次調用中要麼保留代理的軌跡，要麼被動地訪問原材料，或是任務級別的策略。它們都未能保留我們認為對於重複相同上下文工作負載最需要的：可重用的定向知識（例如，上下文包含什麼、如何組織，以及哪些實體、常量和模式在歷史上曾經有用）關於重複的上下文本身。我們介紹了PEEK，一個將這些定向知識緩存並維護為上下文地圖的系統：一個在代理提示中大小固定的小型工件，為其提供持久的外部上下文預覽。該地圖由一個可編程的緩存策略維護，該策略包含三個模塊：一個提煉器，從推理時信號中提取可轉移的知識；一個製圖者，將其轉換為結構化編輯；以及一個基於優先級的驅逐器，強制執行固定的標記預算。在長上下文推理和信息聚合方面，PEEK在使用93-145次更少的迭代且成本比最先進的提示學習框架ACE低1.7-5.8倍的情況下，超越了強基準6.3-34.0%。在上下文學習方面，PEEK的解決率和評分準確度分別提高了6.0-14.0%和7.8-12.1%，成本比ACE低1.4倍。這些增益在各種LM和代理架構中具有普遍性，包括OpenAI Codex，一個生產級的編碼代理。綜合這些結果顯示，上下文地圖幫助長上下文LLM代理更準確和高效地與重複的外部上下文互動。
2605.19932.author: Zhuohan Gu et.al.
2605.19932.authors: Zhuohan Gu, Qizheng Zhang, Omar Khattab, Samuel Madden
